articles.headline: Meet the AI workers who tell their friends and family to stay away from AI | Artificial intelligence (AI)
articles.description: When the people making AI seem trustworthy are the ones who trust it the least, it shows that incentives for speed are overtaking safety, experts say

articles.headline: The A.I. Boom Is Driving the Economy. What Happens if It Falters?
Higher Education’s AI Problem
articles.description: Across the country, colleges and universities are struggling to figure out how to incorporate AI into the classroom. ChatGPT debuted almost exactly three years ago. And very quickly, students began to see its potential as a study buddy, an immense research tool and, for some, a way to cheat the system.This week on The Sunday Story we look at the rapid growth of AI in higher ed and consider what it means for the future of teaching and learning.

articles.headline: AI rivals like OpenAI, Nvidia, and Oracle are collaborating to build ‘Stargate’—but a Yale expert says it violates 135 years of antitrust law
articles.description: Six AI leaders are uniting to form a single company. How is this possibly legal, asks Madhavi Singh of Yale’s Thurman Arnold Project, an initiative dedicated to antitrust issues.

articles.headline: Does Yann LeCun’s Departure and Rising AI Spend Shift the Bull Case for Meta Platforms (META)?
articles.description: In November 2025, Meta Platforms announced that Yann LeCun, its Vice President & Chief AI Scientist and founder of Facebook AI Research, will depart at the end of the year to launch a new AI startup, marking the exit of a central figure in Meta’s artificial intelligence efforts over the last decade. LeCun’s departure comes as Meta intensifies its AI infrastructure investments and faces heightened scrutiny over capital allocation and leadership stability during a significant technological...

articles.headline: The world’s biggest company just told everyone to chill out
articles.description: Concerns about an AI bubble have been simmering for at least a year and a half. Nvidia’s incredibly strong earnings this past week tried to put those fears to bed. It may not have been enough.

articles.headline: Here's why concerns about an AI bubble are bigger than ever
articles.description: Tech companies are pouring billions into AI chips and data centers. Increasingly, they are relying on debt and risky tactics. Financial analysts are worried there's a bubble that will soon pop.

articles.headline: Analysis | Are we in an AI bubble? Eight charts will help you decide.
Insurers retreat from AI cover as risk of multibillion-dollar claims mounts
articles.description: AIG, Great American and WR Berkley seek permission to limit liability from AI agents and chatbots

articles.headline: How patients are using AI to fight back against denied insurance claims
articles.description: As health insurers increasingly rely on artificial intelligence to process claims, denials have been on the rise. In 2023, about 73 million Americans on Affordable Care Act plans had their claims for in-network services denied, and less than 1% of them tried to appeal. Now, AI is being used to help patients fight back. Ali Rogin speaks with Indiana University law professor Jennifer Oliva for more.
